title: SystemVerilog size (tick) operator with expressions
body: In SystemVerilog the tick operator can be used to change the width of an expression: <const>'( <expression> ) where <expression> will be extended/cropped to <const> bits width; if it was a signed expression and <const> is larger that its natural width, then signum extension will be performed. Now consider this example: `module test ( input [5:0] b1, input [5:0] b2, output [9:0] a1, output [9:0] a2 );` `assign a1 = 10'( b1 ); // Verilator is happy` `assign a2 = 10'( b2-b1 ); // Verilator spits the dummy` `endmodule` In the second assign case Verilator 4.210-19 emits a warning: "Operator SUB expects 10 bits on the LHS but LHS's VARREF b2 generates 6 bits." I do believe that that is incorrect: the tick operator should be applied on the result of the SUB, which has the same width as its operands. It seems that instead of extending the 6 bits wide SUB result to 10 bits, Verilator expects the SUB to return a 10 bits wide result.
